Method of Making.comment: I believe that this was made in Photoshop (or equivalent) software using graphic design and digital collage skills to resemble 3D appearance.

Sound.comment: Touch due to the texture of the meat and harpoons. Taste due to the depiction of the meat.

Emotional Reinforcement.comment: The realistic depiction of the whale and its meat does reinforce the emotional aspect of the data. I think this effect could have been intensified further by focusing more explicitly on the killing of it (if it was looking less alive or more bloody etc).

visualElements.comment: Variations in size represent different quantities. I think that the use of shape resemblance and material to depict a whale also contributes to the encoding (the tail appears visually less significant relative to the head).

visualChannels.comment: The position also support context because there is a meaningful connection the parts of the whale (and their significance).
Cutting the whale into pieces, supports the construction of the size encoding of the marks.

visualElements.comment: The segmenting of the form is part of the data encoding.

The labels that provide the exact amounts have the shape of a harpoon (typically used to hunt fish).
(part of annotation)

contextualAttributes.comment: It has the form of a harpoon for hunting whales
